info.name: Oppizi – Open Charge Map API Tests
info.description:
API test suite for the Open Charge Map v3 API.
Covers GET /poi/ and GET /referencedata/ with response time, status code, schema, and business logic validations.

Setup: Import this collection and the accompanying environment file, then set the `apiKey` variable to your OCM key.

item.name: POI – GET /poi/
POI-01 – Status code is 200
POI-02 – Response time under 1000 ms

exec: pm.test('POI-02 | Response time is under 1000ms', function () {

item.name: POI-03 – Response is a JSON array

exec: pm.test('POI-03 | Response body is a JSON array', function () {

item.name: POI-04 – Schema: required top-level fields present

exec: // Validates each POI object contains the required top-level fields
// defined in the OCM OpenAPI spec (POI schema).
pm.test('POI-04 | Each POI has required top-level fields', function () {

item.name: POI-05 – Schema: AddressInfo structure

exec: // Validates AddressInfo nested object structure per the OCM spec.
pm.test('POI-05 | AddressInfo has correct structure', function () {
// Required AddressInfo fields from OCM spec

item.name: POI-06 – Schema: Connections array structure

exec: pm.test('POI-06 | Connections is an array with valid items', function () {

item.name: POI-07 – Business logic: maxresults is respected

exec: pm.test('POI-07 | Result count does not exceed maxresults', function () {

item.name: POI-08 – Business logic: coordinates are within search radius

exec: // Haversine distance check – every POI returned must be within the requested radius of the search origin.
pm.test('POI-08 | All POIs are within the requested search radius', function () {
// Allow 5 % tolerance for floating-point and projection edge cases
const dist = haversineKm(srcLat, srcLng, addr.Latitude, addr.Longitude);
pm.expect(dist, `POI[${i}] (ID ${poi.ID}) is ${dist.toFixed(2)} km away – exceeds radius`)

item.name: POI-09 – Business logic: countrycode filter is respected

exec: pm.test('POI-09 | All POIs belong to the requested country', function () {
if (!expectedCode) { return; } // skip if not filtered

item.name: POI-10 – Business logic: IDs are unique

exec: pm.test('POI-10 | No duplicate POI IDs in response', function () {
pm.expect(unique.size, `Duplicate IDs found: ${ids.filter((id, i) => ids.indexOf(id) !== i)}`)

item.name: POI-11 – Business logic: latitude and longitude are valid ranges

exec: pm.test('POI-11 | All POI coordinates are within valid geographic ranges', function () {

item.name: POI-12 – Negative: missing API key returns 401

exec: pm.test('POI-12 | Request without API key is rejected (401)', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.include("You must specify an API key using the key query parameter or x-api-key header.");

item.name: POI-13 – Negative: invalid coordinates return empty array

exec: // Coordinates in the middle of the ocean should yield 0 results.
pm.test('POI-13 | Remote ocean coordinates return empty result set', function () {
pm.expect(pois.length, 'Expected 0 results for remote ocean coords').to.equal(0);

item.name: REF-10 – Business logic: StatusTypes contain operational status

exec: // The OCM spec defines that StatusTypes must include at least one entry with IsOperational=true for the POI scan flow to be meaningful.
pm.test('REF-10 | At least one StatusType is marked as operational', function () {

item.name: REF-11 – Business logic: ConnectionType IDs align with POI data

exec: // Step 1 of 2: fetch a sample POI set and store ConnectionTypeIDs.
// Step 2 runs in the test script below once referencedata/ responds.
pm.test('REF-11 | ConnectionTypeIDs used in POIs all exist in reference data', function () {
if (!raw) { pm.expect.fail('Pre-request fetch did not return POI data'); }
